Free Text: The tower is located to the West of the main clock tower stairs and is an open shaft reported to have been used for by the original clock tower bells. From 2011, shaft will be used as a service riser for cable routes and will be installed with access ladders and stages - note that Asbestos containing material has been identified within the current shaft false ceiling at ground level. - Shaft will be designate limited access on completion of project works.

Free Text: ERTMS TEST TRAIN OPERATIONS COMMENCE ON 15 JULY 2013 AND ARE LIKELY TO CONTINUE UNTIL AT LEAST JULY 2017. THIS PROHIBITION IS CLASSIFIED AS TEMPORARY AS IT WILL BE REVIEWED AFTER THE OPERATIONS HAVE FINALLY CEASED; UNTIL THEN IT MUST BE REGARDED AS BEING IN FORCE AT ALL TIMES. THE DOWN HERTFORD LINE <ELR - HDB> FROM WL1950 <21.0940 / 21M 42CH TO WL2201, THE TEST TRAIN LIMIT BOARD, <25.1230 / 25M 55CH> WILL BE USED IN EITHER DIRECTION BY ENIF TEST TRAIN OPERATIONS TRAINS SIGNALLED USING IN-CAB SIGNALLING MOVEMENT AUTHORITIES. IT WAS IDENTIFIED AT ENIF HAZAN WORKSHOPS THAT FOR SAFETY AND CONSISTENCY, THAT THIS PORTION OF LINE SHOULD BE REGARDED AS BEING  BI-DIRECTIONAL AT ALL TIMES. THE TEST TRAIN WILL NOT ADHERE TO CONVENTIONAL SIGNALLING PRINCIPLES OR INSTRUCTIONS FOR WORKING IN THE WRONG DIRECTION. THE PROHIBITION FOR WARNING SSOW WORKING WAS DEEMED BY THE PROJECT AS A MEANS OF ELIMINATING THE RISK OF TRACK WORKERS BEING STRUCK BY EITHER THE TEST OR CONVENTIONAL TRAINS.
